name: Basiliximab
description: A recombinant chimeric (murine/human) monoclonal antibody (IgG1k) that functions as an immunosuppressive agent, specifically binding to and blocking the interleukin-2 receptor a-chain (IL-2R alpha, also known as CD25 antigen) on the surface of activated T-lymphocytes. It is a 144 kDa glycoprotein obtained from fermentation of an established mouse myeloma cell line genetically engineered to express plasmids containing the human heavy and light chain constant region genes and mouse heavy and light chain variable region genes encoding the RFT5 antibody that binds selectively to the IL-2R alpha.
indication: For prophylactic treatment of kidney transplant rejection
mechanismOfAction: Basiliximab binds with high-affinity to the alpha-subunit (CD25) of the high-affinity IL-2 receptor. This inhibits IL-2 binding, which inhibits T-cell activation and prevents the body from mounting an immune response against the foreign kidney.
pharmacodynamics: Basiliximab functions as an IL-2 receptor antagonist. Specifically it inhibits IL-2-mediated activation of lymphocytes, a critical pathway in the cellular immune response involved in allograft rejection.

interactions.name: Natalizumab
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Basiliximab is combined with Natalizumab.

interactions.name: Pimecrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Pimecrolimus is combined with Basiliximab.

interactions.name: Tofacitinib
interactions.description: Basiliximab may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Tofacitinib.

interactions.name: Tacrolimus
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tacrolimus is combined with Basiliximab.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Basiliximab.

interactions.name: Leflunomide
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Basiliximab is combined with Leflunomide.

interactions.name: Fingolimod
interactions.description: Basiliximab may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Fingolimod.

interactions.name: Roflumilast
interactions.description: Roflumilast may increase the immunosuppressive activities of Basiliximab.

interactions.name: Varicella Zoster Vaccine (Live/Attenuated)
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Varicella Zoster Vaccine (Live/Attenuated) can be decreased when used in combination with Basiliximab.

interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Yellow fever vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Basiliximab.
